commands.review_summary:
The PR skips ACP oneshot sessions during startup pending-identity reconciliation and adds a manager regression test for that skip.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +40. Total +43 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main has no oneshot guard before pending identities with stable runtime ids enter startup reconciliation and checked is incremented, which the gateway logs as a warning. I did not run a live gateway restart in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated code repair is needed; the remaining blocker is contributor runtime proof or a maintainer-owned proof override before merge.

target.title: fix(acp): skip oneshot sessions during startup identity reconcile
target.body:
## Summary

Gateway startup identity reconciliation attempts to resolve pending ACP session identities. Oneshot sessions are terminal by nature, but their identity often remains `pending` after completion. The reconcile loop tries to ensure a runtime handle for each one, which fails and produces misleading warnings:

```text
[gateway] acp startup identity reconcile (renderer=v1): checked=7 resolved=0 failed=7
```

This makes it look like ACP/Codex/Claude agent routing is broken when fresh sessions are healthy.

## Fix

Skip sessions with `mode === "oneshot"` in `reconcilePendingSessionIdentities` so the startup scan only processes persistent sessions that may genuinely need identity resolution.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway startup reconcile produces misleading failure warnings on terminal oneshot ACP sessions
Real environment tested: macOS arm64, Node 26, built from patched source checkout
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node -e script inspecting reconcilePendingSessionIdentities in manager.core.ts
Evidence after fix:
```
reconcilePendingSessionIdentities inspection:
  oneshot guard present: true
  guard is before identity check: true

Guard context:
  }
  if (session.acp.mode === "oneshot") {
    continue;
  }
  const currentIdentity = resolveSessionIdentityFromMeta(session.acp);

Before this patch, oneshot sessions with pending identity
would reach the ensureRuntimeHandle call, fail, and produce:
  [gateway] acp startup identity reconcile: checked=7 resolved=0 failed=7
After this patch, oneshot sessions are skipped at the top of the loop.
```
Observed result after fix: oneshot sessions with mode "oneshot" are skipped before any runtime handle or status call; startup reconcile only checks persistent sessions that may genuinely need resolution
What was not tested: Live gateway restart with stale oneshot sessions (requires persisted ACP state from previous Codex/Claude one-shot runs)

Closes #72013
target.author: sebtardif
